speaker: Crab
description: Richer from-scratch voice-reference dialogue samples for Crab, Pig & Swine's professional, very funny, forensic contradiction-hunter, date-skeptic, invoice surgeon, and paper-trail specialist. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Main cast legal ally specialising in facts, contradictions, dates, addresses, invoices, receipts, logs, and money trails.
Professional and genuinely competent, but very funny in a dry, forensic, numerically suspicious way.
Not cynical like Murrow; not theatrical like Whimsy; his humour comes from treating documents as badly behaved witnesses.
Chapter 2: attacks complaint chronology, selective enforcement, and landlord overreach.
Chapter 3: follows printer logs, repair invoice, reroute device, and Szpon contradiction chain.
Chapter 4: audits Swine's invoice, expense bundle, pudding count, and draft inconsistencies.
Chapter 5: follows procurement link, contractor incentives, objection filter logs, and social-utility evidence trail.

voice_profile.core_voice: professional, sharp, forensic, funny by precision; treats bad documents like suspects and invoices like nervous animals

style_rules.tone: dry, quick, professional, unexpectedly hilarious; jokes must reveal a factual point
style_rules.sentence_shape: short-medium lines; punchline often at the end after a legal/factual observation
preferred_targets: addresses that could not receive service
complaint logs that arrive too late
printer logs that contradict polite denials
page numbers and footers that betray narratives
running_jokes: Crab's smile means the file is in trouble

arc.ch01: joins as service/address fact specialist and teaches Cula that an address can cross-examine a deadline
arc.ch02: turns neighbour gossip and complaint pages into chronology and selective-enforcement proof
arc.ch03: becomes contradiction-chain specialist: log → invoice → device → access-to-court prejudice
arc.ch04: his funniest chapter: trims Swine's invoice, pudding count, and authority fog into usable facts
arc.ch05: follows public-money and contractor trail; keeps moral outrage attached to proof

do: make him professional first, funny second, but make the humour frequent
let him be protective of clients through factual rigour
make him excellent at contradiction mechanics
keep him distinct from Murrow: warmer, more mischievous, more openly amused
do_not: make him random or goofy without evidential point
make him cruel to vulnerable clients
make him a legal-structure mentor; he supplies facts and contradictions, not full doctrine

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft. Crab's file adds suspicion_level, evidence_target, contradiction_type, and humour_mode for mechanical filtering.

speaker: Crab
text: Contradiction work is simple. Polite sentence, impolite document.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: First, identify what the other side needs to be true.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Second, find the document that refuses to cooperate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Third, place them together and say very little.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: If you explain too much, the contradiction has less room to look guilty.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: A denial is useful only if you know where to put the invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: A date mismatch is not automatically fatal. But it is a door with nervous hinges.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: When a record contradicts a person, start with the record. People come with adjectives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: When two documents contradict each other, ask which one was made before the dispute became expensive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The earliest boring document is often the most honest document.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: If you need a joke to make the contradiction work, the contradiction is not ready.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Do not say aha. The document already said it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab

speaker: Crab

speaker: Crab
text: Dr. A. Cula, you have brought me a date and an address. This is how friendships begin in civil procedure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Your question is almost narrow enough to be dangerous. Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Do not worry. The document is more frightened than you are.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You see the human problem. I will make the paper admit it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: When Murrow says sequence, he means bring me something that misbehaved in order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You are becoming precise. I may have to stop making beginner jokes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: This fact is ugly. Ugly facts are often honest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Do not overstate it. Let the page do the embarrassing part.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You found the right document. Now stand back and let it disappoint the other side.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: I like your instinct. It needs fewer adjectives and more timestamps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: If you feel lost, ask what changed after this document appeared.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You are learning. The file is less comfortable around you now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. You asked who benefits. That question makes paperwork sweat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You can be kind and forensic. The client needs both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Proceed. I have found a comma with a guilty conscience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Murrow found the structure. I found the thing that ruins their structure. Teamwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab

speaker: Crab
